title: Generative model for reciprocity and community detection in networks

introduction.importance: Affects development of exchange, power, trust, and solidarity in social relationships
introduction.problem: Community structure alone may not explain reciprocity in complex scenarios; existing models either focus on community structure or reciprocity but not both
introduction.solution: Novel probabilistic latent variable approach that captures both community structure and reciprocity

results.key_challenge: Relaxing the conditional independence assumption between edges
results.scalability: Optimized for sparse datasets and scalable to large datasets
previous_works: Garlaschelli and Loffredo's model for reciprocity and general hidden variable models
Pair-dependent stochastic block model of Holland et al. (limited to hard membership and unweighted networks)

features: Relaxes conditional independence assumption between edges

contributions: Novel probabilistic latent variable approach that captures both community structure and reciprocity
Likelihood function optimized for sparse datasets and scalability to large datasets
Relaxing the conditional independence assumption between edges
limitations_of_previous_models: Models with community structure fail to reproduce observed reciprocity values
Models capturing reciprocity don't account for community structure
Holland et al.'s model is limited to hard membership and unweighted networks
